messages.content:
The embedded software world contains its challenges. Compilers support different
revisions of the C Standard. They ignore requirements in places, sometimes to
make the language more usable in some special regard. Sometimes it's to simplify
their support. Sometimes it's due to specific quirks of the microcontroller they
are targeting. Simulators add another dimension to this menagerie.

Unity is designed to run on almost anything that is targeted by a C compiler. It
would be awesome if this could be done with zero configuration. While there are
some targets that come close to this dream, it is sadly not universal. It is
likely that you are going to need at least a couple of the configuration options
described in this document.

All of Unity's configuration options are `#defines`. Most of these are simple
definitions. A couple are macros with arguments. They live inside the
unity_internals.h header file. We don't necessarily recommend opening that file
unless you really need to. That file is proof that a cross-platform library is
challenging to build. From a more positive perspective, it is also proof that a
great deal of complexity can be centralized primarily to one place to
provide a more consistent and simple experience elsewhere.

In summary, the challenges of embedded software development include the variety of C Standard revisions and compiler quirks, as well as the added complexity of simulators. However, Unity aims to simplify the process by offering configuration options through `#define` statements in the `unity_internals.h` header file. While zero configuration would be ideal, it is likely that some configuration options will be needed for specific targets. Overall, building a cross-platform library like Unity is a complex task, but by centralizing complexity, it can provide a more consistent and streamlined experience for developers.
翻译中文
嵌入式软件开发的挑战包括C标准修订版和编译器怪癖的多样性，以及模拟器的额外复杂性。然而，Unity旨在通过在`unity_internals.h`头文件中提供`#define`语句的配置选项来简化这个过程。尽管零配置是理想的，但很可能需要针对特定目标进行一些配置选项。总的来说，构建像Unity这样的跨平台库是一个复杂的任务，但通过集中复杂性，它可以为开发人员提供更一致和简洁的体验。

It doesn't matter if you're using a target-specific compiler and a simulator or
a native compiler. In either case, you've got a couple choices for configuring
these options:

1. Because these options are specified via C defines, you can pass most of these
options to your compiler through command line compiler flags. Even if you're
using an embedded target that forces you to use their overbearing IDE for all
configuration, there will be a place somewhere in your project to configure
defines for your compiler.
2. You can create a custom `unity_config.h` configuration file (present in your
toolchain's search paths). In this file, you will list definitions and macros
specific to your target. All you must do is define `UNITY_INCLUDE_CONFIG_H` and
Unity will rely on `unity_config.h` for any further definitions it may need.

无论您使用特定目标的编译器和模拟器还是本地编译器，都有几种选择来配置这些选项：

1. 由于这些选项是通过C定义指定的，因此可以通过命令行编译器标志将大多数选项传递给编译器。即使您使用迫使您在所有配置方面都使用其过度控制的IDE的嵌入式目标，也会在项目中某处设置编译器定义的地方。
2. 您可以创建一个自定义的`unity_config.h`配置文件（存在于您工具链的搜索路径中）。在此文件中，您将列出特定于目标的定义和宏。您所需做的只是定义`UNITY_INCLUDE_CONFIG_H`，Unity将依赖于`unity_config.h`以获取可能需要的任何其他定义。

If you've been a C developer for long, you probably already know that C's
concept of an integer varies from target to target. The C Standard has rules
about the `int` matching the register size of the target microprocessor. It has
rules about the `int` and how its size relates to other integer types. An `int`
on one target might be 16 bits while on another target it might be 64. There are
more specific types in compilers compliant with C99 or later, but that's
certainly not every compiler you are likely to encounter. Therefore, Unity has a
number of features for helping to adjust itself to match your required integer
sizes. It starts off by trying to do it automatically.

如果您已经成为了C开发人员很长一段时间，那么您可能已经了解到C的整数概念因目标而异。C标准有关于`int`匹配目标微处理器的寄存器大小的规则，以及有关于`int`及其大小与其他整数类型的关系的规则。在一个目标上，`int`可能是16位，而在另一个目标上，它可能是64位。更具体的类型位于符合C99或更高版本的编译器中，但这肯定不是您可能遇到的每个编译器。因此，Unity具有许多功能来帮助调整以匹配所需的整数大小，它首先尝试自动进行调整。

If you've disabled both of the automatic options above, you're going to have to
do the configuration yourself. Don't worry. Even this isn't too bad... there are
just a handful of defines that you are going to specify if you don't like the
defaults.
如果您已禁用上面的两个自动选项，则需要自己进行配置。不要担心。即使如此，这也不是太难...如果您不喜欢默认值，只需指定几个定义即可。

Define this to be the number of bits an `int` takes up on your system. The
default, if not autodetected, is 32 bits.
将其定义为您系统上`int`所占的位数。如果没有自动检测，则默认值为32位。

Define this to be the number of bits a `long` takes up on your system. The
default, if not autodetected, is 32 bits. This is used to figure out what kind
of 64-bit support your system can handle. Does it need to specify a `long` or a
`long long` to get a 64-bit value. On 16-bit systems, this option is going to be
ignored.
将其定义为您系统上`long`所占的位数。如果没有自动检测，则默认值为32位。此定义用于确定您的系统可以处理什么样的64位支持。需要指定`long`还是`long long`来获取64位值。在16位系统上，此选项将被忽略。

Define this to be the number of bits a pointer takes up on your system. The
default, if not autodetected, is 32-bits. If you're getting ugly compiler
warnings about casting from pointers, this is the one to look at.
将其定义为您系统上指针所占的位数。如果没有自动检测，则默认值为32位。如果您收到有关指针转换的丑陋编译器警告，则应查看此定义。

Unity will automatically include 64-bit support if it auto-detects it, or if
your `int`, `long`, or pointer widths are greater than 32-bits. Define this to
enable 64-bit support if none of the other options already did it for you. There
can be a significant size and speed impact to enabling 64-bit support on small
targets, so don't define it if you don't need it.
如果Unity自动检测到64位支持，或者您的int、long或指针宽度大于32位，则Unity将自动包括64位支持。如果没有其他选项已经为您启用64位支持，则定义此选项以启用64位支持。在小型目标上启用64位支持可能会带来显着的大小和速度影响，因此如果不需要，不要定义此选项。

In the embedded world, it's not uncommon for targets to have no support for
floating point operations at all or to have support that is limited to only
single precision. We are able to guess integer sizes on the fly because integers
are always available in at least one size. Floating point, on the other hand, is
sometimes not available at all. Trying to include `float.h` on these platforms
would result in an error. This leaves manual configuration as the only option.
在嵌入式领域中，很常见的情况是目标根本没有对浮点运算的支持，或者只支持单精度。我们能够即时猜测整数大小，因为整数至少始终有一个大小可用。另一方面，有时根本没有浮点运算可用。在这些平台上尝试包含`float.h`将导致错误。这只能通过手动配置来解决。

Unity aims for as small of a footprint as possible and avoids most standard
library calls (some embedded platforms don’t have a standard library!). Because
of this, its routines for printing integer values are minimalist and hand-coded.
Therefore, the display of floating point values during a failure are optional.
By default, Unity will print the actual results of floating point assertion
failure (e.g. ”Expected 4.56 Was 4.68”). To not include this extra support, you
can use this define to instead respond to a failed assertion with a message like
”Values Not Within Delta”. If you would like verbose failure messages for floating
point assertions, use these options to give more explicit failure messages.

Unity的目标是尽可能地减小占用空间，并避免使用大部分标准库调用（一些嵌入式平台没有标准库！）。因此，其整数值打印例程是极简主义和手工编码的。因此，在失败时显示浮点值是可选的。默认情况下，Unity将打印浮点断言失败的实际结果（例如“Expected 4.56 Was 4.68”）。如果不包含此额外的支持，则可以使用此定义，而更改为响应失败的断言时显示消息，例如“Values Not Within Delta”。如果您需要针对浮点运算的详细故障消息，请使用这些选项以提供更明确的故障消息。

If enabled, Unity assumes you want your `FLOAT` asserts to compare standard C
floats. If your compiler supports a specialty floating point type, you can
always override this behavior by using this definition.
如果启用，Unity则假定您希望您的`FLOAT`断言比较标准的C浮点数。如果您的编译器支持一种特殊的浮点类型，则始终可以使用此定义覆盖此行为。

If you look up `UNITY_ASSERT_EQUAL_FLOAT` and `UNITY_ASSERT_EQUAL_DOUBLE` as
documented in the big daddy Unity Assertion Guide, you will learn that they are
not really asserting that two values are equal but rather that two values are
"close enough" to equal. "Close enough" is controlled by these precision
configuration options. If you are working with 32-bit floats and/or 64-bit
doubles (the normal on most processors), you should have no need to change these
options. They are both set to give you approximately 1 significant bit in either
direction. The float precision is 0.00001 while the double is 10-12.
For further details on how this works, see the appendix of the Unity Assertion
Guide.

如果您查看在Unity断言指南中记录的`UNITY_ASSERT_EQUAL_FLOAT`和`UNITY_ASSERT_EQUAL_DOUBLE`，您将了解它们并不是真正断言两个值相等，而是断言两个值“足够接近”相等。 “足够接近”由这些精度配置选项控制。如果您使用32位浮点数和/或64位双精度浮点数（大多数处理器上的正常情况），则应该没有必要更改这些选项。它们都设置为在任何方向上给您大约1个有效位。 float精度为0.00001，而double为10-12。有关如何工作的更多详细信息，请参见Unity Assertaion Guide的附录。

Unity uses the `NULL` macro, which defines the value of a null pointer constant,
defined in `stddef.h` by default. If you want to provide
your own macro for this, you should exclude the `stddef.h` header file by adding this
define to your configuration.
Unity 默认使用 `stddef.h` 中定义的 `NULL` 宏，该宏定义了一个空指针常量的值。如果您想为此提供自己的宏，可以通过将此定义添加到您的配置中来排除`stddef.h`头文件。

In addition to the options listed above, there are a number of other options
which will come in handy to customize Unity's behavior for your specific
toolchain. It is possible that you may not need to touch any of these... but
certain platforms, particularly those running in simulators, may need to jump
through extra hoops to run properly. These macros will help in those
situations.
除上面列出的选项外，还有许多其他选项可用于定制Unity以适应特定的工具链。您可能不需要调整其中任何选项...但是某些平台，特别是在模拟器中运行的平台，可能需要额外的配置才能正常运行。这些宏将在这些情况下提供帮助。

For some targets, Unity can make the otherwise required setUp() and tearDown()
functions optional. This is a nice convenience for test writers since setUp and
tearDown don’t often actually do anything. If you’re using gcc or clang, this
option is automatically defined for you. Other compilers can also support this
behavior, if they support a C feature called weak functions. A weak function is
a function that is compiled into your executable unless a non-weak version of
the same function is defined elsewhere. If a non-weak version is found, the weak
version is ignored as if it never existed. If your compiler supports this feature,
you can let Unity know by defining UNITY_WEAK_ATTRIBUTE or UNITY_WEAK_PRAGMA as
the function attributes that would need to be applied to identify a function as
weak. If your compiler lacks support for weak functions, you will always need to
define setUp and tearDown functions (though they can be and often will be just
empty). You can also force Unity to NOT use weak functions by defining
UNITY_NO_WEAK. The most common options for this feature are:

对于某些目标，Unity可以使通常需要的setUp()和tearDown()函数变为可选。这对于测试编写人员来说是很方便的，因为setUp和tearDown通常实际上并不做任何事情。如果您使用gcc或clang，那么这个选项将自动为您定义。如果其他编译器支持一个名为弱函数特性的C特性，则也可以支持此行为。弱函数是一个编译为可执行文件的函数，除非同一函数的非弱版本在其他地方定义。如果找到非弱版本，则弱版本将被忽略，就好像它从来不存在过一样。如果您的编译器支持此功能，则可以通过定义UNITY_WEAK_ATTRIBUTE或UNITY_WEAK_PRAGMA作为函数属性来通知Unity。如果您的编译器不支持弱函数，则始终需要定义setUp和tearDown函数（尽管它们可以是并且通常将是空函数）。您也可以通过定义UNITY_NO_WEAK来强制Unity不使用弱函数。此特征的最常见选项为：

Some compilers require a custom attribute to be assigned to pointers, like
`near` or `far`. In these cases, you can give Unity a safe default for these by
defining this option with the attribute you would like.
在某些编译器中，需要将指针
